text.title: Meta Audience Types: Core, Custom, Lookalike
text.summary: Definitions, setup steps, and selection rules for the three Meta audience types: Core (cold), Custom (warm), and Lookalike (scale).

section.title: Core Audiences
section.p: Core audiences target cold traffic by demographics, interests, and behaviors. Components: Location, Age, Gender, Languages, Interests (hobbies/brands/topics), Behaviors (purchase behavior, device use), Demographics (job title, education, life events).

rule.statement: Minimum Core audience size is 500K; ideal range is 1-5M. Below 500K, CPM spikes and delivery becomes constrained.
rule.rationale: Meta's ad auction requires sufficient audience depth to find the right users at a competitive price. Too narrow and the algorithm cannot optimize; CPM rises sharply and reach plateaus.

section.p: Interest targeting strategy: start with 5-10 related interests. Finding relevant interests: competitor brands, industry publications, related tools the audience uses, communities they belong to.

section.title: Custom Audiences
section.p: Custom audiences use first-party data: website pixel events, email lists, app activity, video engagement, lead form interactions, Facebook/Instagram page engagement.

rule.statement: Install and verify the Meta Pixel with standard events before creating any website custom audience.
rule.rationale: Without pixel events firing correctly, website audiences will not populate or will include the wrong users. Verify event completeness in Events Manager before launch.

section.p: Website audience segments ranked by intent: Purchasers (180d) → Add to cart (7d) → Pricing page (14d) → Product page (30d) → Blog visitors (60d) → All visitors (180d).

rule.statement: Always create exclusion audiences (purchasers, active subscribers) before running acquisition campaigns.
rule.rationale: Showing acquisition ads to existing customers wastes spend and degrades ad relevance scores. Exclusions also prevent the algorithm from optimizing toward already-converted users who inflate conversion metrics without representing net-new customers.

section.title: Lookalike Audiences
section.p: Lookalike audiences let Meta find users similar to a source audience. Percentages: 1% = highest quality/smallest, 2-3% = balanced, 5-10% = scale/lower quality.

rule.statement: Use purchasers or high-value customers as the Lookalike source, not all website visitors.
rule.rationale: The source audience defines the match pattern. Visitors include bounced traffic and irrelevant browsers; purchasers are the highest-quality signal. A LAL from visitors finds people who might visit; a LAL from purchasers finds people who might buy.

list.item: Best source audiences ranked: 1) Purchasers/customers, 2) High-value customers, 3) Leads who converted, 4) Website visitors with action, 5) Email subscribers.
Minimum source size: 1,000 people. Ideal: 2,000-10,000. More data = better matching.
Strategy: LAL_Purchasers_1% for best quality, LAL_Purchasers_2-3% for balance, LAL_Purchasers_5-10% for scale.
